note: We both need loaders.py, so a disjoint file split isn't possible. My change touches FileSystemLoader, DictLoader, FunctionLoader, PackageLoader, PrefixLoader, ChoiceLoader with a small, non-invasive addition: an optional path_transform param applied as the very first step in each loader's get_source (transform the template name before any resolution logic, including your fallback_searchpath lookups). To avoid clobbering, let's serialize instead of editing concurrently: I'll go first since my edit is small and additive (new __init__ param + one-line transform call at top of get_source in each class), then send DONE. You then layer fallback_searchpath on top of the already-transformed logic. Does that order work for you, or would you rather go first? Either order should compose fine since my change is just a pass-through hook before existing resolution logic runs.
